menstrual.name: Rest Mode
menstrual.one_liner: Your body's doing a lot right now — rest isn't laziness, it's literally what this phase is for.

nutrition.focus: Replenish what your body is losing — iron, warmth, and anti-inflammatory foods are your best friends right now.
avoid: caffeine (worsens cramps and inflammation)
alcohol (depletes iron and tanks your mood)
cold or raw foods (warm, cooked meals are easier on your body right now)
nutrition.tip_card: A simple dal with spinach does more for you this week than any supplement. Your body's literally working overtime — iron is your best friend right now.

avoid: HIIT
heavy weightlifting
intense cardio
exercise.tip_card: Movement is medicine this week — but gentle movement. Even a 20-minute walk outside can shift your whole mood. You don't need to earn rest.

mood.expect: Low energy, a pull toward quiet and solitude, maybe some irritability or sadness — this is your most inward phase and it's completely normal.
mood.tip_card: If you want to cancel plans and stay in bed with a hot water bottle, that's not drama — that's your body asking for what it needs. Listen to it.

partner.phase_summary: She's in her rest phase — her body needs warmth, low-key energy, and zero pressure right now.
do: Suggest cozy, low-effort plans (movie night, takeout at home)
Bring warmth — literally and figuratively (hot tea, a heating pad goes a long way)
Check in gently without expecting a lot of conversation
Handle logistics or errands without being asked
avoid: Big social events or crowded places
Heavy or confrontational conversations
Commenting on her energy level or mood
Expecting her to match your energy
partner.date_idea: Cozy movie night at home — let her pick, order her favorite food, and just be present without an agenda.

follicular.name: Build Mode
follicular.one_liner: That rising energy you're feeling? It's real — your hormones are climbing and your brain is genuinely sharper right now.

nutrition.focus: Support rising estrogen with seeds, fermented foods, and fresh produce — your gut and hormones are closely linked this phase.
avoid: excess processed sugar (disrupts the hormonal momentum you've got going)
ultra-processed snacks
nutrition.tip_card: Pumpkin seeds and flaxseeds in your follicular phase aren't just trendy — they genuinely support the estrogen your body is building. Throw them in your smoothie, yogurt, or salad.

recommended: HIIT (energy is building — start testing it)
avoid: overtraining (energy is rising but you're not at peak yet — pace yourself)
exercise.tip_card: This is the best time to start something new or go a little harder than usual. Your body recovers faster and your motivation is higher — use it.

mood.expect: Rising energy, sharper focus, creative ideas flowing, more social — you might feel like a newer, lighter version of yourself after the menstrual phase.
mood.tip_card: That urge to plan, create, and connect? Lean into it. This is your brain at one of its most receptive — great week to tackle anything that needs fresh thinking.

partner.phase_summary: She's got rising energy and a social spark — great week to make plans and try something new together.
do: Suggest plans and new experiences — she'll be up for them
Make reservations, plan a weekend trip, float an idea
Match her energy and be present and engaged
This is a great week for deeper conversations — she's open and connected
avoid: Being passive or disengaged — she's in social mode and wants connection
partner.date_idea: Try a restaurant you've both been meaning to visit or sign up for an activity together (a class, a day hike, a market run) — she's curious and up for it.

ovulation.name: Peak Mode
ovulation.one_liner: This is your superpower window — you're at your most energetic, magnetic, and clear-headed all month.

nutrition.focus: Help your body process peak estrogen efficiently — cruciferous vegetables and anti-inflammatory foods are key this phase.
avoid: excess alcohol (disrupts hormone balance at your hormonal peak)
refined sugar and processed foods
nutrition.tip_card: Cruciferous veg (broccoli, cauliflower, cabbage) are the unsung heroes of ovulation week — they help your liver clear excess estrogen so you feel good instead of bloated and wired.

exercise.tip_card: If you've been building toward a fitness goal — a PR, a new class, a longer run — this is your week. Your pain tolerance is higher and your performance is at its monthly peak.

mood.expect: High confidence, magnetic energy, communication comes easily, more extroverted than usual — you might feel like you can take on the world, and honestly, kind of yes.
mood.tip_card: You're at your most persuasive and socially magnetic right now — great week for a big presentation, an important conversation, or anything that needs you at full power.

partner.phase_summary: She's at her energetic and social peak this week — confident, magnetic, and ready for an adventure.
do: Compliment her genuinely — she's radiating and she knows it
Say yes to her ideas and energy
Make an effort — she'll notice and appreciate it
avoid: Cancelling plans last minute — this is when she most wants to be out in the world
Low-effort evenings when she's clearly in peak energy mode
partner.date_idea: An adventure date — a hike with a view, a concert, exploring a new neighborhood, or anything with a little spontaneity. Match her peak energy.

luteal.name: Protect Mode
luteal.one_liner: Your body is winding down and asking for kindness — the slower pace is intentional, not a setback.

nutrition.focus: Stabilize mood and energy with magnesium-rich foods and complex carbs — your serotonin naturally dips this phase and food can genuinely help.
avoid: caffeine (amplifies PMS anxiety and irritability — even if it feels necessary)
excess salt (worsens bloating)
alcohol (significantly worsens mood swings and sleep)
nutrition.tip_card: Dark chocolate and pumpkin seeds together are your luteal phase secret weapon — magnesium genuinely helps with the mood dip and the cravings. Have them without guilt.

recommended: anything that genuinely feels good to you
avoid: forcing HIIT or intense workouts if your body isn't feeling it
punishing yourself for lower performance — your body is in a different gear
exercise.tip_card: Move because it genuinely feels good, not because you think you should. Even a slow walk outside changes your mood more than you'd expect — but only if you don't force it.

mood.expect: Emotional sensitivity, lower energy as the phase progresses, PMS possible in the second half (days 22-28) — everything feels a bit more, and that's real, not dramatic.
mood.tip_card: If you're feeling more irritable, teary, or anxious than usual — that's the luteal phase, not you falling apart. Name it, give yourself more buffer, and ask for what you need.

partner.phase_summary: She may need extra patience and low-key energy this week — PMS is a real hormonal shift, not a mood she can just snap out of.
do: Lead with patience, especially in the second half of the week
Handle logistics without being asked — it's the most loving thing you can do
Check in with 'what do you need?' instead of assuming
Keep plans low-pressure and easy to cancel if she's not up to it
avoid: Big confrontational or emotionally heavy conversations — wait for a better week
Commenting on her mood or telling her to cheer up
Making her feel like she's being dramatic or difficult
Piling on social commitments
partner.date_idea: Her favorite low-key dinner — either cooking something comforting at home or her go-to local restaurant. Comfort and familiarity over novelty this week.
